title: cynara cardunculus var. hortensis
intro: Cynara cardunculus var. hortensis, commonly known as cardoon, is a perennial thistle plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cynara cardunculus var. hortensis include its ability to support liver function, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed as dietary supplements or used topically to promote skin and wound healing.
